title: sisymbrium acutangulum subsp. chrysanthum
intro: Sisymbrium acutangulum subsp. chrysanthum, also known as the yellow-horseshoe or golden mustard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Sisymbrium acutangulum subsp. chrysanthum include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce blood pressure. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
